output:
# solution.py
"""TeamCollaborationManager - an in-memory team collaboration backend.

This module implements a thread-safe, dependency-aware collaboration system
that lets a team work together on shared projects.  It provides:

* **User registration and authentication-grade identity** - accounts with
  unique usernames/emails and roles.
* **Project management** - projects owned by users with start/end dates.
* **Task management** - tasks that belong to a project, can be assigned,
  scheduled with deadlines, and moved through a lifecycle.
* **Messaging** - threaded messages and file attachments scoped to a project
  or a task (real-time collaboration feed).
* **Performance dashboards** - completion rates, average completion time, and
  peer ratings.
* **Reporting** - project progress, team performance, and individual reports,
  exportable to CSV and PDF.

Design notes
------------
The public surface is the :class:`TeamCollaborationManager` facade.  It owns a
single shared :class:`_SharedState` (one ``RLock`` + one global id counter +
all storage) and delegates to six cooperating services
(``users``, ``projects``, ``tasks``, ``messages``, ``performance``,
``reports``).  Every service shares the *same* lock object so that cross-entity
operations are atomic and id generation is globally unique and monotonic.

All accessor methods return deep copies of the stored entities so that callers
can never mutate internal state by holding on to a returned object.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Sequence, Tuple


# ===========================================================================
# Exceptions
# ===========================================================================
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation rules."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with existing related state."""


# ===========================================================================
# Enumerations
# ===========================================================================
class UserRole(Enum):
    """Roles a user can hold within the team."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states for a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for reports."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """The kinds of reports the system can produce."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ===========================================================================
# Entities
# ===========================================================================
@dataclass
class User:
    """A registered team member."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A project owned by a user with a planned schedule."""

    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work belonging to a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus
    assignee_id: Optional[int]
    deadline: Optional[datetime]
    started_at: Optional[datetime]
    completed_at: Optional[datetime]
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A binary file attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A message in a project or task discussion thread."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int]
    task_id: Optional[int]
    parent_id: Optional[int]
    attachments: List[Attachment]
    created_at: datetime
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating from one user to another."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str
    task_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime


# ===========================================================================
# Shared state
# ===========================================================================
def _default_clock() -> datetime:
    """Return the current wall-clock time (used when no clock is injected)."""

    return datetime.now()


class _SharedState:
    """Central storage shared by every service.

    Holds the single lock, the monotonic id counter, the injectable clock and
    all entity dictionaries.  Keeping everything here guarantees that the
    ``_lock`` object referenced by each service is identical and that ids are
    globally unique across entity types.
    """

    def __init__(self, now) -> None:
        self.lock = threading.RLock()
        self.now = now if now is not None else _default_clock
        self._next_id = 0

        self.users: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self.projects: Dict[int, Project] = {}
        self.tasks: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self.messages: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self.ratings: Dict[int, Rating] = {}

    # -- id generation -----------------------------------------------------
    def next_id(self) -> int:
        """Return a process-unique, monotonically increasing id starting at 1."""

        with self.lock:
            self._next_id += 1
            return self._next_id

    # -- internal accessors (return live objects, never copies) ------------
    def require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message


# ===========================================================================
# Validation helpers
# ===========================================================================
def _require_non_empty(value: str, label: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
        raise ValidationError("{0} must be a non-empty string".format(label))
    return value


def _validate_email(email: str) -> str:
    if not isinstance(email, str) or not email.strip():
        raise ValidationError("Email must be a non-empty string")
    candidate = email.strip()
    at = candidate.find("@")
    # Require a single '@' with content on both sides.
    if at <= 0 or at != candidate.rfind("@") or at == len(candidate) - 1:
        raise ValidationError("Email '{0}' is invalid".format(email))
    return candidate


def _clone(obj):
    """Return a deep copy so callers cannot mutate stored state."""

    return copy.deepcopy(obj)


def _clone_list(objs) -> list:
    return [copy.deepcopy(o) for o in objs]


# ===========================================================================
# Base service
# ===========================================================================
class _Service:
    """Common base giving every service the shared lock and state."""

    def __init__(self, state: _SharedState) -> None:
        self._state = state
        self._lock = state.lock


# ===========================================================================
# User service
# ===========================================================================
class _UserService(_Service):
    def register(self, username: str, email: str,
                 role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER) -> User:
        _require_non_empty(username, "Username")
        clean_email = _validate_email(email)
        if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
            raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

        with self._lock:
            self._ensure_unique_username(username, exclude_id=None)
            self._ensure_unique_email(clean_email, exclude_id=None)
            user = User(
                id=self._state.next_id(),
                username=username,
                email=clean_email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._state.now(),
            )
            self._state.users[user.id] = user
            return _clone(user)

    def get(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._state.require_user(user_id))

    def list(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            users = sorted(self._state.users.values(), key=lambda u: u.id)
            return _clone_list(users)

    def update(self, user_id: int, username: Optional[str] = None,
               email: Optional[str] = None,
               role: Optional[UserRole] = None) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._state.require_user(user_id)

            if username is not None:
                _require_non_empty(username, "Username")
                self._ensure_unique_username(username, exclude_id=user_id)
            new_email = None
            if email is not None:
                new_email = _validate_email(email)
                self._ensure_unique_email(new_email, exclude_id=user_id)
            if role is not None and not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            if username is not None:
                user.username = username
            if new_email is not None:
                user.email = new_email
            if role is not None:
                user.role = role
            return _clone(user)

    def delete(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(user_id)

            for project in self._state.projects.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError(
                        "User {0} owns project {1}".format(user_id, project.id)
                    )
            for task in self._state.tasks.values():
                if (task.assignee_id == user_id
                        and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED):
                    raise ConflictError(
                        "User {0} has open task {1}".format(user_id, task.id)
                    )

            del self._state.users[user_id]

    # -- helpers -----------------------------------------------------------
    def _ensure_unique_username(self, username: str,
                                exclude_id: Optional[int]) -> None:
        for user in self._state.users.values():
            if user.id != exclude_id and user.username == username:
                raise DuplicateError(
                    "Username '{0}' is already taken".format(username)
                )

    def _ensure_unique_email(self, email: str,
                             exclude_id: Optional[int]) -> None:
        for user in self._state.users.values():
            if user.id != exclude_id and user.email == email:
                raise DuplicateError(
                    "Email '{0}' is already registered".format(email)
                )


# ===========================================================================
# Project service
# ===========================================================================
class _ProjectService(_Service):
    def create(self, name: str, start_date: datetime, end_date: datetime,
               owner_id: int, description: str = "") -> Project:
        _require_non_empty(name, "Project name")
        self._validate_dates(start_date, end_date)
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(owner_id)
            project = Project(
                id=self._state.next_id(),
                name=name,
                description=description or "",
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                created_at=self._state.now(),
            )
            self._state.projects[project.id] = project
            return _clone(project)

    def get(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._state.require_project(project_id))

    def list(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            projects = sorted(self._state.projects.values(), key=lambda p: p.id)
            return _clone_list(projects)

    def update(self, project_id: int, name: Optional[str] = None,
               description: Optional[str] = None,
               start_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
               end_date: Optional[datetime] = None) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._state.require_project(project_id)

            if name is not None:
                _require_non_empty(name, "Project name")
            new_start = start_date if start_date is not None else project.start_date
            new_end = end_date if end_date is not None else project.end_date
            self._validate_dates(new_start, new_end)

            if name is not None:
                project.name = name
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            return _clone(project)

    def delete(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_project(project_id)

            doomed_tasks = [
                t.id for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id
            ]
            doomed_task_set = set(doomed_tasks)
            for task_id in doomed_tasks:
                del self._state.tasks[task_id]

            doomed_messages = [
                m.id for m in self._state.messages.values()
                if m.project_id == project_id or m.task_id in doomed_task_set
            ]
            for message_id in doomed_messages:
                del self._state.messages[message_id]

            del self._state.projects[project_id]

    # -- helpers -----------------------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _validate_dates(start_date: datetime, end_date: datetime) -> None:
        if start_date is None or end_date is None:
            raise ValidationError("Project requires start and end dates")
        if end_date < start_date:
            raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")


# ===========================================================================
# Task service
# ===========================================================================
class _TaskService(_Service):
    def create(self, project_id: int, title: str,
               assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
               deadline: Optional[datetime] = None) -> Task:
        _require_non_empty(title, "Task title")
        with self._lock:
            project = self._state.require_project(project_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._state.require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "deadline cannot be before the project start date"
                )
            task = Task(
                id=self._state.next_id(),
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                started_at=None,
                completed_at=None,
                created_at=self._state.now(),
            )
            self._state.tasks[task.id] = task
            return _clone(task)

    def get(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._state.require_task(task_id))

    def list_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [
                t for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id
            ]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return _clone_list(tasks)

    def list_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = [
                t for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                if t.assignee_id == user_id
            ]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return _clone_list(tasks)

    def assign(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._state.require_task(task_id)
            self._state.require_user(assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            return _clone(task)

    def reassign(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        # Reassignment shares assignment semantics but reads as intent.
        return self.assign(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._state.require_task(task_id)
            task.assignee_id = None
            return _clone(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int,
                     deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._state.require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._state.require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError(
                        "deadline cannot be before the project start date"
                    )
            task.deadline = deadline
            return _clone(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
            raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
        with self._lock:
            task = self._state.require_task(task_id)
            now = self._state.now()

            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now

            task.status = status
            return _clone(task)


# ===========================================================================
# Message service
# ===========================================================================
class _MessageService(_Service):
    def post(self, author_id: int, body: str,
             project_id: Optional[int] = None,
             task_id: Optional[int] = None,
             parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
             attachments: Optional[Sequence[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None
             ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "Provide exactly one of project_id or task_id"
                )
            if project_id is not None:
                self._state.require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._state.require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._state.require_message(parent_id)

            attachment_objs = self._build_attachments(attachments, author_id)
            if not (body or "").strip() and not attachment_objs:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "Message must have a body or at least one attachment"
                )

            message = Message(
                id=self._state.next_id(),
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body or "",
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=attachment_objs,
                created_at=self._state.now(),
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self._state.messages[message.id] = message
            return _clone(message)

    def edit(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._state.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "Only the author may edit this message"
                )
            if not (new_body or "").strip() and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "Message must have a body or at least one attachment"
                )
            message.body = new_body or ""
            message.edited_at = self._state.now()
            return _clone(message)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int,
                       filename: str, data: bytes) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._state.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "Only the author may attach files to this message"
                )
            _require_non_empty(filename, "Filename")
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data or b""),
                    uploaded_by=uploader_id,
                    uploaded_at=self._state.now(),
                )
            )
            return _clone(message)

    def list_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_project(project_id)
            messages = [
                m for m in self._state.messages.values()
                if m.project_id == project_id
            ]
            messages.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return _clone_list(messages)

    def list_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_task(task_id)
            messages = [
                m for m in self._state.messages.values()
                if m.task_id == task_id
            ]
            messages.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return _clone_list(messages)

    def delete(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._state.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "Only the author may delete this message"
                )
            del self._state.messages[message_id]

    # -- helpers -----------------------------------------------------------
    def _build_attachments(self, attachments, author_id) -> List[Attachment]:
        result: List[Attachment] = []
        if not attachments:
            return result
        now = self._state.now()
        for entry in attachments:
            try:
                filename, data = entry
            except (TypeError, ValueError):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "Each attachment must be a (filename, data) pair"
                )
            _require_non_empty(filename, "Filename")
            result.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data or b""),
                    uploaded_by=author_id,
                    uploaded_at=now,
                )
            )
        return result


# ===========================================================================
# Performance service
# ===========================================================================
class _PerformanceService(_Service):
    def completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for(user_id)
            return self._completion_rate(tasks)

    def average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for(user_id)
            return self._average_completion_time(tasks)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id: int, ratee_id: int, score: int,
                      feedback: str = "",
                      task_id: Optional[int] = None) -> Rating:
        if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
            raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
        if score < 1 or score > 5:
            raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(rater_id)
            self._state.require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("Users cannot rate themselves")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._state.require_task(task_id)
            rating = Rating(
                id=self._state.next_id(),
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._state.now(),
            )
            self._state.ratings[rating.id] = rating
            return _clone(rating)

    def ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": _clone_list(ratings),
            }

    def user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)

            status_counts = self._status_counts(tasks)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": len(tasks),
                "completed_tasks": status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED],
                "in_progress_tasks": status_counts[TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS],
                "not_started_tasks": status_counts[TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED],
                "completion_rate": self._completion_rate(tasks),
                "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(tasks),
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # -- shared computation helpers ---------------------------------------
    def _tasks_for(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return [
            t for t in self._state.tasks.values()
            if t.assignee_id == user_id
        ]

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id: int) -> List[Rating]:
        ratings = [
            r for r in self._state.ratings.values()
            if r.ratee_id == user_id
        ]
        ratings.sort(key=lambda r: r.id)
        return ratings

    @staticmethod
    def _status_counts(tasks: Sequence[Task]) -> Dict[TaskStatus, int]:
        counts = {status: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
        for task in tasks:
            counts[task.status] += 1
        return counts

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_rate(tasks: Sequence[Task]) -> float:
        if not tasks:
            return 0.0
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        return completed / len(tasks)

    @staticmethod
    def _average_completion_time(tasks: Sequence[Task]) -> Optional[float]:
        durations = [
            (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
            for t in tasks
            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            and t.started_at is not None
            and t.completed_at is not None
        ]
        if not durations:
            return None
        return sum(durations) / len(durations)


# ===========================================================================
# Report service
# ===========================================================================
class _ReportService(_Service):
    def __init__(self, state: _SharedState,
                 performance: _PerformanceService) -> None:
        super().__init__(state)
        self._performance = performance

    def project_progress(self, project_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_project(project_id)
            now = self._state.now()
            tasks = [
                t for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id
            ]

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee: Dict[int, dict] = {}
            completed = 0
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                elif task.deadline is not None and task.deadline < now:
                    overdue += 1

                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id,
                        {"total": 0, "completed": 0, "in_progress": 0,
                         "not_started": 0},
                    )
                    bucket["total"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed"] += 1
                    elif task.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                        bucket["in_progress"] += 1
                    else:
                        bucket["not_started"] += 1

            total = len(tasks)
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance(self, project_id: Optional[int] = None) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._state.require_project(project_id)
                scoped = [
                    t for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                    if t.project_id == project_id
                ]
            else:
                scoped = list(self._state.tasks.values())

            user_ids = sorted({
                t.assignee_id for t in scoped if t.assignee_id is not None
            })

            rows = []
            for user_id in user_ids:
                user = self._state.users.get(user_id)
                user_tasks = [t for t in scoped if t.assignee_id == user_id]
                ratings = self._performance._ratings_for(user_id)
                rating_count = len(ratings)
                rating_average = (
                    sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                    if rating_count else 0.0
                )
                rows.append({
                    "user_id": user_id,
                    "username": user.username if user else "",
                    "total_tasks": len(user_tasks),
                    "completed_tasks": sum(
                        1 for t in user_tasks
                        if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    ),
                    "completion_rate":
                        _PerformanceService._completion_rate(user_tasks),
                    "average_completion_time":
                        _PerformanceService._average_completion_time(
                            user_tasks),
                    "rating_count": rating_count,
                    "rating_average": rating_average,
                })

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._state.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = [
                t for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                if t.assignee_id == user_id
            ]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)

            status_counts = _PerformanceService._status_counts(tasks)
            ratings = self._performance._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count else 0.0
            )

            task_rows = [
                {
                    "id": t.id,
                    "project_id": t.project_id,
                    "title": t.title,
                    "status": t.status.value,
                    "assignee_id": t.assignee_id,
                    "deadline": t.deadline,
                }
                for t in tasks
            ]
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": len(tasks),
                "completed_tasks": status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED],
                "in_progress_tasks": status_counts[TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS],
                "not_started_tasks": status_counts[TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED],
                "completion_rate":
                    _PerformanceService._completion_rate(tasks),
                "average_completion_time":
                    _PerformanceService._average_completion_time(tasks),
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
                "tasks": task_rows,
            }

    def export(self, report: dict, report_format: ReportFormat):
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
        if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat")

        if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._to_csv(report)
        return self._to_pdf(report)

    # -- serialization helpers --------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _to_csv(report: dict) -> str:
        buffer = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(buffer)
        rtype = report.get("report_type")

        if rtype == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            columns = [
                "user_id", "username", "total_tasks", "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate", "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count", "rating_average",
            ]
            writer.writerow(columns)
            for row in report.get("rows", []):
                writer.writerow([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])

        elif rtype == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            writer.writerow(["metric", "value"])
            writer.writerow(["project_id", report.get("project_id", "")])
            writer.writerow(["total_tasks", report.get("total_tasks", 0)])
            writer.writerow(
                ["completion_rate", report.get("completion_rate", 0.0)]
            )
            writer.writerow(["overdue_tasks", report.get("overdue_tasks", 0)])
            for status_value, count in report.get("status_counts", {}).items():
                writer.writerow(["status_" + str(status_value), count])

        elif rtype == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            writer.writerow(
                ["task_id", "project_id", "title", "status",
                 "assignee_id", "deadline"]
            )
            for task in report.get("tasks", []):
                writer.writerow([
                    task.get("id", ""),
                    task.get("project_id", ""),
                    task.get("title", ""),
                    task.get("status", ""),
                    task.get("assignee_id", ""),
                    task.get("deadline", ""),
                ])

        else:
            keys = list(report.keys())
            writer.writerow(keys)
            writer.writerow([_stringify(report[k]) for k in keys])

        return buffer.getvalue()

    @staticmethod
    def _to_pdf(report: dict) -> bytes:
        rtype = report.get("report_type", "report")
        lines = ["Report type: {0}".format(rtype)]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, _stringify(value)))
        return _render_pdf("Team Collaboration Report", lines)


def _stringify(value) -> str:
    if isinstance(value, (dict, list)):
        return str(value)
    if isinstance(value, datetime):
        return value.isoformat()
    return str(value)


def _render_pdf(title: str, lines: Sequence[str]) -> bytes:
    """Build a minimal, structurally valid single-page PDF document."""

    def escape(text: str) -> str:
        return (
            text.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                .replace("(", "\\(")
                .replace(")", "\\)")
        )

    content_ops = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "50 750 Td", "16 TL"]
    rendered = [title] + list(lines)
    for index, line in enumerate(rendered):
        safe = escape(str(line))
        if index == 0:
            content_ops.append("({0}) Tj".format(safe))
        else:
            content_ops.append("T*")
            content_ops.append("({0}) Tj".format(safe))
    content_ops.append("ET")
    stream = "\n".join(content_ops).encode("latin-1", "replace")

    objects = [
        b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
        b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
        (b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
         b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"),
        (b"<< /Length " + str(len(stream)).encode("ascii") + b" >>\nstream\n"
         + stream + b"\nendstream"),
        b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
    ]

    pdf = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
    offsets: List[int] = []
    for number, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
        offsets.append(len(pdf))
        pdf += "{0} 0 obj\n".format(number).encode("ascii")
        pdf += body
        pdf += b"\nendobj\n"

    xref_position = len(pdf)
    size = len(objects) + 1
    pdf += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(size).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
    for offset in offsets:
        pdf += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"trailer\n"
    pdf += "<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\n".format(size).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"startxref\n"
    pdf += "{0}\n".format(xref_position).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"%%EOF"
    return bytes(pdf)


# ===========================================================================
# Facade
# ===========================================================================
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Thread-safe facade coordinating all collaboration services.

    Parameters
    ----------
    now:
        Optional zero-argument callable returning the current
        :class:`datetime`.  Injecting a deterministic clock makes behaviour
        fully reproducible; when omitted the system clock is used.
    """

    def __init__(self, now=None) -> None:
        state = _SharedState(now)
        self._state = state
        self._lock = state.lock

        self.users = _UserService(state)
        self.projects = _ProjectService(state)
        self.tasks = _TaskService(state)
        self.messages = _MessageService(state)
        self.performance = _PerformanceService(state)
        self.reports = _ReportService(state, self.performance)

    # -- user management ---------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username: str, email: str,
                      role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER) -> User:
        return self.users.register(username, email, role)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        return self.users.get(user_id)

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        return self.users.list()

    def update_user(self, user_id: int, username: Optional[str] = None,
                    email: Optional[str] = None,
                    role: Optional[UserRole] = None) -> User:
        return self.users.update(user_id, username=username, email=email,
                                 role=role)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        return self.users.delete(user_id)

    # -- project management ------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(self, name: str, start_date: datetime,
                       end_date: datetime, owner_id: int,
                       description: str = "") -> Project:
        return self.projects.create(name, start_date, end_date, owner_id,
                                    description)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        return self.projects.get(project_id)

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        return self.projects.list()

    def update_project(self, project_id: int, name: Optional[str] = None,
                       description: Optional[str] = None,
                       start_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
                       end_date: Optional[datetime] = None) -> Project:
        return self.projects.update(project_id, name=name,
                                    description=description,
                                    start_date=start_date, end_date=end_date)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        return self.projects.delete(project_id)

    # -- task management ---------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(self, project_id: int, title: str,
                    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
                    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.create(project_id, title, assignee_id=assignee_id,
                                 deadline=deadline)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.get(task_id)

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return self.tasks.list_by_project(project_id)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return self.tasks.list_by_assignee(user_id)

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.assign(task_id, assignee_id)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.reassign(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.unassign(task_id)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int,
                     deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.set_deadline(task_id, deadline)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.update_status(task_id, status)

    # -- messaging ---------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(self, author_id: int, body: str,
                     project_id: Optional[int] = None,
                     task_id: Optional[int] = None,
                     parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
                     attachments: Optional[Sequence[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None
                     ) -> Message:
        return self.messages.post(author_id, body, project_id=project_id,
                                  task_id=task_id, parent_id=parent_id,
                                  attachments=attachments)

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int,
                     new_body: str) -> Message:
        return self.messages.edit(message_id, editor_id, new_body)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int,
                       filename: str, data: bytes) -> Message:
        return self.messages.add_attachment(message_id, uploader_id, filename,
                                            data)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        return self.messages.list_by_project(project_id)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        return self.messages.list_by_task(task_id)

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        return self.messages.delete(message_id, requester_id)

    # -- performance dashboard ---------------------------------------------
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        return self.performance.completion_rate(user_id)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        return self.performance.average_completion_time(user_id)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id: int, ratee_id: int, score: int,
                      feedback: str = "",
                      task_id: Optional[int] = None) -> Rating:
        return self.performance.submit_rating(rater_id, ratee_id, score,
                                              feedback=feedback,
                                              task_id=task_id)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        return self.performance.ratings_summary(user_id)

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        return self.performance.user_dashboard(user_id)

    # -- reports -----------------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> dict:
        return self.reports.project_progress(project_id)

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id: Optional[int] = None) -> dict:
        return self.reports.team_performance(project_id)

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        return self.reports.individual_performance(user_id)

    def export_report(self, report: dict, report_format: ReportFormat):
        return self.reports.export(report, report_format)


__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "UserRole",
    "TaskStatus",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Attachment",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]


# ===========================================================================
# Small demonstration (only runs when executed directly)
# ===========================================================================
def _demo() -> None:  # pragma: no cover - illustrative only
    from datetime import timedelta

    mgr = TeamCollaborationManager()
    owner = mgr.register_user("ada", "ada@example.com", UserRole.ADMIN)
    worker = mgr.register_user("grace", "grace@example.com")
    start = datetime.now()
    project = mgr.create_project("Apollo", start, start + timedelta(days=30),
                                 owner.id, "Moon shot")
    task = mgr.create_task(project.id, "Design rocket", assignee_id=worker.id)
    mgr.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
    mgr.post_message(owner.id, "Great start!", task_id=task.id)
    mgr.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
    mgr.submit_rating(owner.id, worker.id, 5, "Excellent work")

    dashboard = mgr.get_user_dashboard(worker.id)
    print("Dashboard for {0}: {1}".format(worker.username, dashboard))
    report = mgr.project_progress_report(project.id)
    print("Project progress: {0}".format(report))
    print(mgr.export_report(report, ReportFormat.CSV))


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    _demo()
